title: Fecal incontinence documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports relevant medical history, contributory factors, physical and emotional impact, general examination, anorectal examination only if performed, cognition, and warning context.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult baseline section supports bowel symptoms, contributory factors, physical, emotional, social, cognitive, and functional impact plus clinician-performed examination only when entered.

source.title: Faecal incontinence in adults: management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: Adults reporting faecal incontinence; neurological, obstetric, postoperative, prolapse, cancer-warning, and acute spinal contexts need tailored assessment.
source.setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
source.exclusions: Supports physical, emotional, social, bowel, medical, cognitive, and clinician-performed anorectal assessment documentation without defaulting an intimate examination or generating treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports relevant medical history, contributory factors, physical and emotional impact, general examination, anorectal examination only if performed, cognition, and warning context.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-entered symptom review, prior management, specialist assessment status, preferences, dignity, and follow-up documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact review section supports existing investigation context, prior management, preferences, dignity, specialist status, and clinician-entered follow-up without generating treatment.
